rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SMITH VS. KARDENER FAMILY REVOCABLE TRUST ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday May 6, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT PEI ASSET POOL V, LLC's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained to the causes of action for violation of Cal. Govt Code 12900 et seq and CC 51 et. seq for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions regarding the applicable time of violation and the facts giving rise to the perceived disability claim. Demurrer to the cause of action for IIED is overruled. Outrageousness of the conduct is an issue of fact and Defendant failed to cite any cases for the proposition that under the facts of this case a determination can be made as a matter of law. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: NAIM JAMALI VS. JORGE ISABEL VILLATA ORDONEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday May 6, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANTs KATHERINE ORDONEZ REYES, JORGE ORDONEZ VILLATA, JOSE GUZMAN SINTA and ALBA REYES SANDOVAL's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT This complaint is based on two distinct claims arising out of service of two notices of termination of tenancy. Demurrer to the claim/cause of action based on non-payment of rent is sustained without leave to amend. Based on the allegations in the complaint and exhibits thereto Plaintiff failed to comply with CCP 1179.04(d)(1) and CCP 1179.03(a)(1) and (4). All subsections referenced above were in effect on December 4, 2020 and January 19, 2021. Plaintiff does not suggest in opposition that compliance with these subsections may be alleged in good faith. Demurrer to the claim/cause of action based on lease violation is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to reconcile allegations regarding the number of occupants (notice vs. complaint); regarding the starting date of accrual of daily damages (19g); regarding 3 day notice (12). Demurrer based on all other arguments advanced by the defendant is overruled. Plaintiff shall have 5 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
